---
title: "Maximum Likelihood Estimation"
description: "Likelihood principle, score equations, asymptotic theory, and nonlinear models."
---

## Motivation {#sec-mle-motivation}

*This chapter is under development.* It will show how maximum likelihood provides a general estimation framework beyond linear regression and underpins models like logit and probit.

### What You Will Learn

- How likelihood links model assumptions to estimation
- Score, Hessian, and first-order conditions
- Consistency and asymptotic normality of MLE
- Why MLE and OLS coincide in the Gaussian linear model

---

::: {.callout-note}
## Coming Soon

This chapter will include:

- **Intuition** — “Most plausible parameter” as an estimation rule.
- **Formalization** — Log-likelihood, score vector, information matrix.
- **Proofs** — MLE asymptotics under regularity conditions (collapsed by default).
- **Interactive code** — Likelihood surface explorer for normal and binary-choice models.
:::
